incorrects.description: Student clicked Data Set A on first attempt

steps.text: Look at the ones place: 0, 5, 5, 0. Those ARE 0 or 5. Which data set has different ones digits?

incorrects.description: Student clicked Data Set A on second attempt

params.description: Data Set B panel pulses briefly.

steps.text: Data Set A ends in 0 or 5. Look at Data Set B. The ones digits are 7, 2, 9, 3. None are 0 or 5.

incorrects.description: Student clicked Data Set A three or more times. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Data Set A panel highlights briefly.

steps.text: Let me show you. Data Set A has 20, 35, 55, 80. The ones digits are 0, 5, 5, 0. All are 0 or 5.
Data Set B has 7, 12, 19, 23. The ones digits are 7, 2, 9, 3. None are 0 or 5. That is the signal for Scale of 2. Click Data Set B.
